meanings.definition: Produced a very loud, harsh, and powerful sound, often suddenly or continuously.
examples: Music blared from the speakers late into the night.
The alarm was blaring throughout the building.
Car horns blare constantly in the busy streets.
The television blared loudly while everyone was trying to sleep.

meanings.definition: Displayed or announced something very loudly, clearly, or in an attention-grabbing way.
examples: The newspaper blared shocking headlines across the front page.
Bright advertisements blared from every screen in the market.
The signs were blaring warnings about the storm.
News channels blared updates throughout the evening.

meanings.definition: A loud, harsh, or unpleasant sound.
examples: A sudden blare from the siren startled everyone.
The blares of traffic filled the narrow street.
They woke up to the blare of loud music outside.
The trumpet’s blare echoed through the hall.
